summary: This short post combines two unrelated items: a recommendation of Clay Shirky's essay on group dynamics and online communities, and an announcement that Joel has created an experimental Echo (later Atom) feed using CityDesk. The Shirky quote highlights how online community problems are predictable and recurring, yet builders keep repeating the same mistakes. Joel also positions himself as an early adopter of the Echo syndication format, noting its rapidly evolving specification.
key_points: Clay Shirky's insight that group dynamics problems in online communities are predictable and well-documented, yet repeatedly ignored by new builders
Joel created an early experimental Echo/Atom feed using CityDesk 2.0 beta, positioning himself as a first-mover on the new syndication format
The Echo format was still rapidly changing, reflecting the messy early days of web syndication standards
money_quotes: if you don't accept them upfront, they'll happen to you anyway
And then you'll end up writing one of those documents that says 'Oh, we launched this and we tried it, and then the users came along and did all these weird things.'
Even though you didn't read the thing that was written in 1978.
key_insight: The problems that plague online communities and software projects are well-documented and predictable, yet each generation of builders insists on learning them the hard way.
